themes.heading: Latest update
themes.text: Still current at:22 July 2026 Updated:14 April 2026 Latest update:We continue to advise exercise normal safety precautions in Denmark. Depending on why you're travelling and how long you plan to stay, you may require a visa for travel to the Schengen area. Check the European Entry and Exit (EES) and visa rules before you travel. If you're a dual national of a European country, check whether the EES applies to you and what passport you should enter on. There may be delays at border crossings (see ‘Travel’). We advise: Exercise normal safety precautions in Denmark. Download Denmark Download Europe

themes.heading: Safety
themes.text: Denmark's domestic terror threat level is set to 'significant'. Terrorist attacks could occur anywhere and at any time. Terrorists may target tourist areas and attractions or other places frequented by foreigners. Public transport is also a possible target. Take official warnings seriously and follow the advice of local authorities. Pickpockets often target people in tourist areas. Take care of your belongings, especially on public transport, at national landmarks, museums, railway stations and restaurants. Gang-related crime and civil unrest can happen in Copenhagen, especially in Nørrebro/Nordvest area and Christiania. Full travel advice: Safety

themes.heading: Health
themes.text: The standard of medical facilities is similar to Australia. Major hospitals are in Copenhagen, Aarhus and the Odense area. Full travel advice: Health

themes.heading: Local laws
themes.text: Penalties for all drug offences are severe. Even possession of small amounts can attract heavy fines, jail or immediate deportation. Penalties for vandalism, such as graffiti, may include fines, imprisonment or deportation. Penalties for drink driving or speeding may include heavy fines or jail. There are restrictions on flying drones. Contact the Danish Civil Aviation and Railway Authority for more information and guidance on registration. Full travel advice: Local laws

themes.heading: Travel
themes.text: Denmark is a part of the Schengen area which means you can enter Denmark without a visa in some cases. In other situations, you'll need a visa. If you’re travelling to a country in the Schengen area for a short stay (90 days or less) using an Australian passport, you’ll be required to register your biometric details, such as fingerprints and a photo, when you arrive. You don’t need to take any action before you arrive at the border, and there is no cost for EES registration. There may be delays at border crossings. Depending on why you're travelling and how long you plan to stay, you may require a visa for travel to the Schengen area. Check the EES and visa rules before you travel. If you're a dual national of a European country, check whether the EES applies to you and what passport you should enter on. The currency is the Danish Krone (DKK). Full travel advice: Travel

themes.heading: Local contacts
themes.text: The Australian Government aims to provide high quality consular assistance. There are limits to what we can do. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. For consular help, contact the Australian Embassy, Copenhagen. To stay up to date with local information, follow the Embassy's social media accounts. Full travel advice: Local contacts

themes.heading: Terrorism
themes.text: Terrorism is a threat worldwide. Terrorist attacks could occur anywhere and at any time. Be alert to possible threats, especially in public places. Denmark's domestic terror threat level is set to 'significant' (level 4 of 5). The Danish Government has security measures in place, including at airports and major train stations. The threat of right-wing extremism is rising, and ISIL-inspired extremism continues to be a threat. In the past few years, authorities have arrested several people suspected of planning terrorist attacks. Terrorists are likely to keep planning attacks. Terrorists have staged attacks in several European cities. Targets have included: aviation public transport and transport hubs sporting venues places of mass gathering, including those popular with travellers European security services have also stopped some planned attacks. If there’s a terrorist incident, leave the area if it’s safe to do so and follow the instructions of local authorities. More information: Terrorism Danish Security and Intelligence Service (English)

themes.heading: Crime
themes.text: Serious crime is low. There have been incidents of gang-related crime in Copenhagen, especially in Nørrebro/Nordvest area and Christiania. Pickpockets and bag snatchers often work together, targeting people in tourist areas, including hotel lobbies and public transport in and around Copenhagen. To protect yourself from crime: watch your belongings on public transport, at national landmarks, museums, railway stations and restaurants, especially at night don't go out alone after dark or to isolated places, especially on foot always keep your vehicle and accommodation locked be alert to suspicious behaviour.

themes.heading: Civil unrest and political tension
themes.text: Civil unrest is rare and protests are generally peaceful. However, public protests and events that draw large groups of people can turn violent. Avoid demonstrations and follow the advice of local authorities. Monitor the media and plan your travel to avoid affected areas. More information: Protests and civil unrest

themes.heading: Tours and Adventure Activities
themes.text: There are many adventure activities in Denmark, including in Greenland and the Faroe Islands. To stay safe: check you have everything you need for adventure activities, including the right equipment ensure you're fit enough to do these activities always let people know where you're going follow local advice and check weather forecasts. The weather can be unpredictable, even during the summer months. Check your travel insurance covers these activities. Include coverage for helicopter rescue and medical evacuation. Tour operators don't always follow safety and maintenance standards. If you plan to do a tour or adventure activity: ask about and insist on minimum safety requirements always use available safety gear, such as life jackets or seatbelts if proper safety equipment isn't available, use another provider Polar bears have killed and injured travellers in Greenland. Don't approach polar bears.

themes.heading: Extreme weather and natural disasters
themes.text: Extreme weather can impact your travel. Monitor local media for updates. Although the main peninsular of Denmark is not prone to extreme weather or natural disasters, travellers to Greenland or the Faroe Islands should plan accordingly. Some places in the Arctic are a long way from mobile coverage and help, including: search and rescue evacuation medical facilities. Search and rescue teams in the region are highly skilled. However, help depends on the weather and sea conditions in an emergency. If you need assistance, you may have to wait a long time. If you need to be rescued or evacuated in Greenland, the search and rescue operation could cost you thousands of dollars. Plan accordingly. If severe weather affects the area you're visiting: confirm plans and activities with your tour operator or travel provider check the condition of buildings and facilities with local tour operators and hotels. Register with the Global Disaster Alert and Coordination System to receive alerts on major disasters.

themes.heading: Travel insurance
themes.text: Get comprehensive travel insurance before you leave and closely read the Product Disclosure Statement (PDS) for what is and isn’t covered. It must cover all medical costs overseas, including evacuation. The Australian Government will not pay for these costs. If you can’t afford travel insurance, you shouldn’t travel. This is true for everyone, even if you are healthy and fit. If you don’t have insurance, you might have to pay thousands of dollars up front for medical care. Before you travel, check your policy, make sure: you know what activities and medical care your insurance covers, including evacuations your insurance covers you for your whole trip, including any stopovers you know any exclusions – for example, you might need to pay extra for activities like motorcycling, snow sports, scuba diving or bungee jumping. More information: Travel insurance

themes.heading: Physical and mental health
themes.text: Travelling overseas can be stressful and uncertain. You may experience unfamiliar environments, travel disruptions, changes in normal routines and limited access to health care. Not taking your prescribed medications is a common cause of mental ill-health for Australians overseas. If you are concerned about your welfare, or someone else's, contact your nearest Australian embassy, high commission, or consulate. You can also call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on +61 2 6261 3305. Mental health resources: Information and advice on mental illness (HealthDirect). Living with mental illness (BeyondBlue) Medicare Mental Health (the Department of Health and Aged Care) More information: General health advice Mental health support overseas

themes.heading: Health risks
themes.text: Health risks are broadly similar to those in Australia. Health risks in the Arctic can include: frostbite dehydration severe sunburn snow blindness hypothermia Ask your doctor about health risks and vaccines before you go. More information: Travel to Antarctica and the Arctic Infectious diseases

themes.heading: Medical care
themes.text: There’s no agreement between Australia and Denmark for free healthcare. Tourists must pay for all medical care, even if they need to stay in hospital or be evacuated. This can be very expensive. Make sure your travel insurance covers you medical evacuations and hospital stays. The Australian Government won't pay for these costs. The standard of medical facilities in the main peninsula of Demark is similar to Australia. There are major hospitals in Copenhagen, Aarhus and the Odense area. English is widely spoken. Costs are similar to, or more than, private treatment in Australia. Medical services and facilities in Greenland and the Faroe Islands are limited. Evacuations can be required for serious illness or injury.

themes.heading: Drugs
themes.text: Penalties for drug possession, use or trafficking, even small amounts, include heavy fines and prison sentences. More information: Carrying or using drugs

themes.heading: Visas and border measures
themes.text: Each country or territory decides its own entry and exit rules, including health border screening measures. To find out what you need to enter a destination, ask their embassy, consulate or immigration office.

themes.heading: Visas
themes.text: The main peninsula of Denmark is part of the Schengen area, along with many other European countries, meaning you can enter Denmark without a visa in some situations. In other situations, you'll need a visa. Greenland and the Faroe Islands aren't part of the Schengen area or the EU. Entry to Greenland and the Faroe Islands without a visa is possible for Visa-free countries. Get a legible entry stamp on your passport when you enter the Schengen area for the first time. Check the Schengen Convention before you travel. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Contact the nearest embassy or consulate of Denmark for details about visas, currency customs and quarantine rules. Make sure you meet all entry and exit conditions. The Australian Government can't help you if you don't.

themes.heading: The European Entry/Exit System (EES)
themes.text: The EES has started registering all non-EU nationals entering and exiting the Schengen area. When you arrive in a country in the Schengen area for a short stay (90 days or less) using an Australian passport, you'll need to: have your fingerprints and photo taken answer Schengen Border Code questions. This will create a digital record that registers your biometric details. If you revisit the Schengen Area within 3 years of creating your digital record, you'll only need to provide your fingerprint and/or photograph at the border on entry and exit. The EES is free. You don't need to do anything before arriving at the border. Be alert to fraudulent websites that say you need to register or pay a fee before you travel to the EU. Only use official EU systems to provide your data. If you're a dual national of a European country, check whether the EES applies to you and what passport you should enter on. This process may cause extended delays at border crossings. Arrive well before your flight departure time. Allow for delays when planning transits and onward travel. Schengen Area trip duration rules still apply.

themes.heading: Trip duration limits
themes.text: Australians can travel to the Schengen area for short trips without a visa for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. You must ensure your total stay in the Schengen area does not exceed this limit. The EES makes it easier for local authorities to see if you have overstayed. Immigration penalties and enforcement for exceeding the limit apply, and vary depending on the country you're in. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Before you travel, contact the nearest embassy or consulate for details on entry requirements. More information: Visas and entry requirements in Europe and the Schengen Area Travel to Europe – Official European Union website

themes.heading: Other formalities
themes.text: There are sporadic border controls between Denmark, Sweden and Germany. Border forces will only grant entry if you can present a valid passport. If you're entering Denmark by boat, a valid passport is a requirement, even if you're entering from another Schengen country. Make sure you: carry your passport when crossing borders, including within the Schengen area monitor border conditions by checking local sources and asking transport providers directly.

themes.heading: Lost or stolen passport
themes.text: Your passport is valuable and can be used by others to commit crimes. Don’t let anyone trick you into giving it to them. Always keep it in a safe place. If your passport is lost or stolen, tell the Australian Government: In Australia: contact the Australian Passport Information Service. Overseas: contact the nearest Australian embassy or consulate.

themes.heading: Passport with ‘X’ gender identifier
themes.text: Australian passports comply with international standards for sex and gender. If your passport has an 'X' in the sex field, we can’t guarantee your entry or transit to other countries. Ask the embassy or consulate of your destinations if they’ll accept your passport with an 'X' gender marker. Check all transit destinations too. More information: LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Money
themes.text: The currency is the Danish Krone (DKK). If you travel between Denmark and any non-EU country, you must declare cash of over 10,000 euros (or equivalent). This covers all forms of currency, not only cash. If you don't declare or give the wrong information on entry or exit, you'll need to pay a fine. You don't need to declare cash if you're travelling to or from another EU country. ATMs are common, and credit cards are accepted in most places. Check if your credit card has 'chip and PIN' technology. Many hotels, restaurants and shops need this.

themes.heading: Travel to Greenland
themes.text: Greenland has no serious personal security concerns, and the crime rate is low. However, the weather is extreme. Flights can often be delayed or cancelled at short notice. You may need to remain in Greenland for some time. There are no roads or railways between towns. Travel is by air, sea, ski, snowmobile or dogsled only. Limited medical facilities are available in Greenland. Patients may be evacuated by helicopter to the capital or neighbouring Iceland for treatment. Search and rescue response may come from many hundreds of kilometres away. They can only provide basic transport and medical care. Make sure you have accessible funds‎ and travel insurance ‎that cover your overseas medical costs, including hospitalisation and medical evacuation.

themes.heading: Travel to the Faroe Islands
themes.text: The Faroe Islands have no serious personal security concerns, and the crime rate is low. However, the weather is extreme. Flights can often be delayed or cancelled at short notice. You may need to remain in the Faroe Islands for some time. Limited medical facilities are available in parts of the Faroe Islands. You may be evacuated to Tórshavn (the capital of the Faroe Islands), neighbouring Iceland, or to the main peninsula of Denmark for treatment. Search and rescue response may come from many hundreds of kilometres away. They can only provide basic transport and medical care. Make sure you have accessible funds‎ and travel insurance ‎that cover your overseas medical costs, including hospitalisation and medical evacuation. More information: Travel to Antarctica and the Arctic

themes.heading: Driving permit
themes.text: To drive, you'll need both: a valid Australian driver's licence (digital licences may not be accepted) an International Driving Permit (IDP). You must get your IDP before leaving Australia. More information: Driving or riding

themes.heading: Road travel
themes.text: Always keep your headlights on. Roads can be dangerous in winter because of ice. Vehicles are required to have winter tyres from 1 November to 31 March. Check local rules. When driving, check cycle lanes before turning. Cyclists often have the right of way. Take care not to walk on cycle lanes. Look both ways before crossing one-way streets, as bicycle traffic is often allowed in both directions. Accidents and injuries resulting from collisions between cyclists and pedestrians are common. E-Scooters are common in larger cities: you don't need a driver's licence to ride them, but you must be over 15 years of age they mustn't be driven on pedestrian footpaths lights are required any time it is in operation you can't take passengers on e-scooters it's mandatory to wear a helmet when riding an e-scooter. More information: Road safety Driving regulations in Denmark Danish Road Traffic Authority

themes.heading: Motorcycles
themes.text: Check if your travel insurance policy covers you when using a motorbike, quad bike or similar vehicle. Always wear a helmet.

themes.heading: Public transport
themes.text: Public transport is efficient and of a high standard. More information: Transport and getting around safely

themes.heading: Sea travel
themes.text: Copenhagen is a hub for cruise travel to the Baltic and the Arctic regions. Before you book a cruise, check the following: experience and reputation of cruise operators standard of onboard medical facilities. You might be going somewhere far from search-and-rescue, evacuation and medical facilities. Check your travel insurance covers you for the type of travel you're doing. Ensure it covers any pre-existing medical conditions. More information: Going on a cruise Travel to Antarctica and the Arctic

themes.heading: Air travel
themes.text: DFAT doesn’t give advice about how safe different airlines or flight paths are. Safety risks and weather events may cause flight delays and cancellations. Contact your travel agent or airline to confirm your plans. Airlines and airports can restrict what you can take in your luggage. Regulations may be different to those in Australia. Check with your airline before you travel. Check Denmark's air safety profile with the Aviation Safety Network. More information: Air travel

themes.heading: Medical emergencies
themes.text: Call 112 If not life-threatening, call +45 1813 to be referred to a hospital.

themes.heading: Consular contacts
themes.text: The Australian Government aims to provide high quality consular assistance. There are limits to what we can do. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas.

themes.heading: Australian Embassy, Copenhagen
themes.text: Dampfaergevej 26 2nd floor 2100 Copenhagen Phone: +45 7026 3676 Email: copenhagen.embassy@dfat.gov.au Website: denmark.embassy.gov.au Facebook: Australia in Denmark, Norway and Iceland X: @AusAmbDK Check the Embassy website for details about opening hours and any temporary closures.

themes.heading: 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre
themes.text: In a consular emergency, if you can't contact an embassy, call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on: +61 2 6261 3305 from overseas 1300 555 135 in Australia

themes.heading: 주재국 신고
themes.text: ㅇ 긴급상황 발생시 (소방서, 경찰, 병원 앰뷸런스 통합) : 112 ㅇ 응급환자 의료상담 - 1813 ㅇ 경찰서 (Politi) - 114 ㅇ 경찰 분실물 신고센터 (Hittegodskontor) +45 38 74 88 22 (전화업무 평일 9:00 – 11:00) ㅇ 코펜하겐 여행자 안내소 +45 70 22 24 42 o 대중교통 - 덴마크 대중교통 연락처 통합 안내 웹사이트 (덴마크 대중교통 내 분실물, 과태료, 연락처 관련 내용 확인 가능) : https://dinoffentligetransport.dk/en/contact-us *버스 : +45 7015 7000 전화하여 1번 누르면 연결 *DSB 기차 : +45 7015 7000 전화하여 2번 누르면 연결 *지하철(Metro) : +45 7015 7000 전화하여 3번 누르면 연결

themes.heading: 의료기관 연락처
themes.text: ㅇ 국립의료 서비스 이용을 위한 통합 연락번호 : 1813 ㅇ 여행 중 (24시간) 응급환자 발생 및 병원 진료 희망시 우선 ☏ 1813 으로 연락하여 현재 상태와 위치 등의 개인정보를 알려주고 방문 가능한 국립병원 (응급실 포함) 을 안내 받습니다. 1813은 영어로 의사소통이 가능하며, 통역지원이 필요할 경우 외교부 영사콜센터 (+82 2 3210 0404) 무료통역 지원서비스를 활용하시기 바랍니다. - 웹사이트 : https://www.regionh.dk/english/healthcare-services/emergency-medical-services/pages/medical-helpline-1813.aspx

themes.heading: 기본정보 (basisinformatie)
themes.text: ㅇ 공식 국가 명칭 : 덴마크 왕국(Kingdom of Denmark / Kongeriet Danmark) ㅇ 위치 : 북위 54~57도, 동경 8~15도에 위치하는 유틀란드 반도 및 406개 섬 ㅇ 수도 : 코펜하겐 ㅇ 면적 : 42,934㎢(한반도의 약 1/5) ※ 그린란드ㆍ패로제도 등 자치령 제외 ㅇ 인구 : 약 600만명('26년) ㅇ 민족 : 아리안계의 덴(Danes)족, 고트(Goth)족이 전체 인구의 90% 이상 ㅇ 언어 : 덴마크어 ㅇ 교육 : 9년간 의무교육(7~16세) ㅇ 종교 : 루터복음교(국교, 75%), 이슬람교, 가톨릭, 침례교, 유대교 등 ㅇ 정부형태 : 입헌군주제하 내각책임제 ㅇ 의회 : 단원제(총 179석, 그린란드와 패로제도에 각 2석씩 할당) ㅇ 국가원수 : 프레데릭 10세(Frederik X) 국왕 (’24.1월 즉위) ㅇ 총리 : 메테 프레데릭센(Mette Frederiksen) (’19.6월-)

themes.heading: 사건ㆍ사고의 유형
themes.text: ※ 코펜하겐 시내 중심가 및 공항, 호텔에서 외국인을 상대로 한 소매치기가 빈번하게 발생되고 있으며, 특히 동양인을 대상으로 한 소지품 소매치기가 자주 발생함 [호텔] ㅇ 체크인하는 사이 가방 도난 사고 발생 ㅇ 호텔 내 식당 조식 뷔페에서 소지품 및 가방을 의자에 놓고 음식을 가지러 간 사이 도난 사고 발생 ㅇ 타인에게 짐을 맡기고 화장실을 다녀온 사이 가방 도난 사고 발생 [지하철 및 기차] ㅇ 낯선 사람이 짐을 짐칸에 올려주겠다고 호의를 베푼 사이 소지품 도난 사고 발생 ㅇ 낯선 사람이 다량의 동전을 바닥에 떨어뜨려 정신을 분산시킨 뒤 도와주는 사이 가방 도난 사고 발생 ㅇ 대중교통 탑승권 판매 자판기를 통해 탑승권 구매시 신용카드로 비밀번호가 노출돼 2차 범죄 피해 발생 [식당] ㅇ 테이블 위에 소지품 (지갑, 핸드폰, 가방 등)을 올려놓고 식사 중 낯선 사람이 다가와 돈을 달라고 구걸하고 이야기를 하고 있는 틈을 타 소지품을 훔쳐 달아나는 사고 발생 [횡단보도] ㅇ 보행 중 신호에 걸려 횡단보도 및 횡단보도 사이에 멈춰있는 사이 가방 안에 든 개인소지품 소매치기 피해 발생 [ATM] ㅇ 카드 비밀번호가 노출돼 2차 범죄 피해 발생 [자동차] ㅇ 귀중품 및 가방 등을 차 안 눈에 띄는 곳에 놓고 주차한 사이 유리창을 깨고 가방을 훔쳐 달아나는 사고 발생

themes.heading: 유의해야 할 지역
themes.text: ㅇ 덴마크는 비교적 치안이 잘되어 있으며 여행에 큰 어려움은 없으나, 수도인 코펜하겐 내에서 소매치기 범죄 피해가 빈번히 발생되고 있어 소지품 (여권 등) 관리에 주의가 요구됩니다. 소매치기 발생 주요 지역은 코펜하겐 내 주요 관광지 (뉘하운(Nyhavn), 아말리엔보(Amalienborg), 코펜하겐 시청(Rådhuspladsen), 티볼리 놀이공원(Tivoli) 등) 와 코펜하겐 중앙역(København H) 인근, 코펜하겐 소재 호텔 및 식당, 공항입니다. 아울러 대중교통 이용시에도 가방 소지에 유의하시기 바랍니다. ㅇ 또한 코펜하겐 내 뇌어브로(Norrebro) 지역에는 불법 범죄단체 간의 범죄 행위가 발생하는 경우가 간혹 있어 방문 시 주의가 필요하며, 중앙역(København H) 기준 남서쪽 지역에 이민자들로부터 발생되는 소매치기 피해가 접수되고 있으니 밤늦은 시간 해당 지역에 혼자 다니는 일은 삼가하는 것이 좋습니다.

themes.heading: 출입국 (in- en uitreis)
themes.text: 무비자 입국 가능 여부(1회 입국시 최대 연속 체류 가능 일자) ㅇ 외교관 여권 소지자 : O, (180일 중 90일) ㅇ 관용여권 소지자 : O, (180일 중 90일) ㅇ 일반여권 소지자(복수와 단수를 구분할 경우 별도 표기 필요) : O, (180일 중 90일) ㅇ 도착사증 발급 여부 : 도착사증 미발급 입국시 유의사항 등 ㅇ 우리 국민 무사증 입국 여행자의 경우, 노르딕 국가(덴마크,스웨덴,노르웨이,핀란드,아이슬란드) 외 쉥겐협약 가입국에 체류하다가 덴마크로 입국했을 경우, 쉥겐협정보다 한-덴마크 양자사증면제협정이 우선 적용됨 - 덴마크를 포함한 노르딕 국가(덴마크, 스웨덴, 노르웨이, 핀란드, 아이슬란드)에서 최근 180일 중 90일 동안 체류 가능 *단기체류 관련 덴마크 이민청 문의 : https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Contact-us/Contact-the-Danish-Immigration-Service 출국시 유의사항 등 ㅇ 우리 국민 무사증 입국 여행자의 경우, 노르딕 국가(덴마크,스웨덴,노르웨이,핀란드,아이슬란드) 외 쉥겐협약 가입국에 체류하다가 덴마크를 통해 출국할 경우, 쉥겐협정보다 한-덴마크 양자사증면제협정이 우선 적용됨 - 덴마크를 포함한 노르딕 국가(덴마크, 스웨덴, 노르웨이, 핀란드, 아이슬란드)에서 최근 180일 중 90일 동안의 체류일 계산 *단기체류 관련 덴마크 이민청 문의 : https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Contact-us/Contact-the-Danish-Immigration-Service 참고자료 ㅇ 주한덴마크대사관 : https://sydkorea.um.dk/ko ㅇ 주덴마크대한민국대사관 : https://overseas.mofa.go.kr/dk-ko/index.do ㅇ 기타 참고자료 ▪ 덴마크 이민청 웹사이트 무비자 여행 안내(우리 국민 대상 안내 포함) - https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Applying/Short%20stay%20visa/Visa%20free%20visits - https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Visum/Visa-(short-term)-%E2%80%93-How-long-are-you-allowed-to-stay-in-Denmark-and-the-other-Schengen-countries'/?anchor=8446985CF31C46F48FB94BE6BE919FC1&callbackItem=E7DB3F82B9C14DCFAA314EB1A72DDEC5&callbackAnchor=2461BA020DAA483D956486ED9E6B46F18446985CF31C46F48FB94BE6BE919FC1

themes.heading: 종교 관련
themes.text: ㅇ 덴마크의 국교는 루터복음교입니다. 기독교 국경일을 공휴일로 지정하고 있으며, 의무교육과정에서 기독교에 대해 배우기 시작하며 덴마크인은 출생과 동시에 루터복음교 교구에 자동 가입이 됩니다.

themes.heading: 음식 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 스뫼어브뢰드(Smørrebrød) : 스뫼어브뢰드(오픈샌드위치)는 덴마크의 가장 대중적인 식사로 호밀빵 위에 각종 야채와 고기 등을 올려먹는 음식입니다. ㅇ 데니쉬 페이스트리(Danish Pastry) : 세계적으로 널리 알려진 페이스트리로 덴마크를 비롯한 북유럽 국가에서 전해졌습니다. ㅇ 뢸그뢸 멜 플뢸(Rødgrød med Fløde) : 덴마크의 대표적인 디저트로 일종의 베리 푸딩입니다. ㅇ 에블스키워(Æbleskiver) : 덴마크 전통의 팬케이크로 주로 크리스마스 시즌에 즐겨 먹습니다.

themes.heading: 팁 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 요금에 팁이 포함되어 있는 경우가 대부분이어서 일반적으로 팁을 별도로 주는 관습은 미미합니다. 그러나 서비스가 특별히 만족스럽거나 요금에 팁이 포함되어 있지 않는 경우에는 7~10% 정도 팁을 줄 수도 있습니다.

themes.heading: 자전거 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 덴마크의 거리에는 정장을 입고, 어린아이를 자전거에 태운 상태로 자전거 도로를 달리는 덴마크인을 쉽게 볼 수 있고, 특히 수도 코펜하겐은 자전거 도시로 유명합니다. 모든 도심 도로에 자전거가 다닐 수 있는 전용도로가 설치되어 있는데, 운전이나 보행시에는 주의가 필요합니다.

themes.heading: 주요 관광 명소
themes.text: ㅇ 티볼리 공원 (Tivoli Gardens) ㅇ 뉘하운 (Nyhavn) ㅇ 크론보르 성 (Kronborg Slot) ㅇ 프레데릭스보르 성 (Frederiksborg Slot) ㅇ 코펜하겐 인어공주 동상 (Statue of the Little Mermaid) [덴마크 유명인사] ㅇ 안데르센 : 동화작가 ㅇ 니콜라이 그룬트비 : 교육가이자 정치인, 종교인 ㅇ 엔리코 달가스, : 농업개혁자, 부흥운동가 ㅇ 쇠렌 키에르케고르 : 철학자 ㅇ 요른 웃손 : 건축가 [인사말] ㅇ 안녕하세요. (아침/점심/저녁때 인사) - God morgen. [고 모-은] - God dag. [고 대‘] - God aften. [고 아프튼] [인사말] ㅇ 안녕하세요. (아침/점심/저녁때 인사) - God morgen. [고 모-은] - God dag. [고 대‘] - God aften. [고 아프튼] [기본 답변] ㅇ 네. / 아니오. / 좋습니다. - Ja. / Nej. / Det er fint. - [야 / 나이 / 데 에아 핀-트] [기본 답변] ㅇ 네. / 아니오. / 좋습니다. - Ja. / Nej. / Det er fint. - [야 / 나이 / 데 에아 핀-트] [기본 단어] ㅇ 전화, 택시, 버스, 병원, 대사관, 불, 물 - telefon, taxa, bus, hospital, Ambassade, brand, vand - [텔레폰, 탁‘사, 부ㅅ, 호스피탤-, 앰배새-ð, 브란‘ㅌ, 밴‘] [감사의 표현] ㅇ 감사합니다. - Tak skal du have. [탁 스캘 두 해] [도움 요청] ㅇ 도와주시기 바랍니다. - Må jeg bede om din hjælp? [모 야이 비‘ 옴 딘 옐’]

themes.heading: 주요 교통 법규 및 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 자동차 운전석 위치는 한국과 동일함 ㅇ 자동차 신호체계는 한국과 큰차이가 없으나, 좌회전·우회전 신호가 없는 경우 직진신호시에 좌회전·우회전을 할 수 있음 ※ 적신호에서 우회전 불가능 ㅇ 차도와 인도 사이에 자전거 신호등과 함께 자전거 도로가 있음 ㅇ 국제운전면허증 인정 ㅇ 덴마크는 한국과 달리 우측 방향 운전 시에도 반드시 초록불을 확인해야 하며 특히, 자전거를 유의해야 함

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 사례
themes.text: ㅇ 보행자가 자전거도로를 인도로 착각하여 자전거도로에서 보행 중 자전거와 일어난 접촉사고 ㅇ 차량 운전자가 우회전시, 차도와 인도 사이에 있는 자전거 도로에서 주행 중인 자전거를 인지하지 못하고 일어난 접촉 사고 ㅇ 주차된 차량 내 물건 절도를 목적으로 한 차량 유리 파손 및 물건 도난 사고

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 발생시 대처방법
themes.text: ㅇ 교통사고 발생시 주재국 경찰 (114) 및 주덴마크대사관에 연락하여 사건을 신고하고, 응급 환자의 경우 주재국 응급실 (1813) 로 연락하여 엠뷸런스 서비스를 제공 받는다. ㅇ 차량내 물건 절도 및 차량 유리 파손 피해시 주재국 경찰(114) 과 주덴마크대사관에 연락하여 해당 사건을 접수하고 랜트카 업체에 연락하여 차량 교체 또는 수리에 대한 요청을 한다. 개인 소유 차량의 경우 개인 차량 보험회사에 연락을 한다. ㅇ 분실신고가 필요한 경우 아래 정보 참고 바랍니다. - 코펜하겐 내 분실신고가 가능한 경찰서 - 코펜하겐 중앙역 경찰서 : Banegårdspladsen 1, 1570 Copenhagen - 코펜하겐 공항 경찰서 : Petersdalvej 1A, 2770 Kastrup - 코펜하겐 경찰 분실물 보관소(Politi Hittegodskontor) : +45-3874-8822 (평일 9:00-14:00)

themes.heading: 대중교통
themes.text: ㅇ 국철, 에스 토그, 메트로, 시내버스는 서로 다른 회사에서 운영하고 있으며, 환승 가능여부에 대한 확인이 사전에 요구됩니다. [덴마크 국철/에스 토그(S-tog)] ㅇ 교외까지 가는 열차와 수도권을 달리는 근교 열차 2종류가 있음 ㅇ www.dsb.dk에서 노선 정보 및 표 구매를 할 수 있음 ㅇ 1등석과 일반석이 구분(국철만 해당) 되어 있으며, 정숙 지역 (silent zone) 칸이 있기 때문에 각별한 주의가 요구 ㅇ 국철 및 Metro의 경우 자전거를 동반할 경우 추가 요금이 부과되나, 에스토그는 무료 ※ 일부 대중교통의 경우 이용객이 많은 출퇴근 시간대에 자전거 동반 탑승이 안될 수 있으니 주의 요망 [지하철(Metro)] ㅇ 코펜하겐 인근 지역에서 운영되는 Metro는 M1-M4까지 총 4호선을 운행하고 있으며 무인 자동 운전 ㅇ 대부분 24시간 운행 ㅇ 자전거나 애완견을 동행한 경우, 추가 표를 구매해야 함 (이용객이 많은 출퇴근 시간대에는 자전거 동반 탑승이 안됨) ㅇ www.m.dk에서 상세한 노선 정보를 확인할 수 있음 [시내버스(Busser)] ㅇ 시내 중심부에서 교외까지 그물망처럼 노선이 이어져 있음 ㅇ 앞쪽 승차구는 두개로 나뉘어 있는데, 오른쪽은 현금으로 티켓을 사는 사람이 이용하고, 왼쪽은 티켓에 시간을 찍을 사람이나 환승하는 사람이 이용함 ㅇ 주요 정류장 몇 개를 제외하고는 차내 안내방송이 없으며, 대부분 운전석 뒤쪽 위 전광판에 역이름이 표시됨 ㅇ www.moviatrafik.dk에서 자세한 노선 정보를 확인할 수 있음 [택시 (Taxa)] ㅇ Uber : 앱 'Uber' 다운로드하여 이용 ㅇ Dan Taxi : 앱 'Dantaxi' 다운로드하여 이용 / 전화번호 +45 48 48 48 48 ㅇ 4X35 Taxa : 앱 'TAXA 4x35' 다운로드하여 이용 / 전화번호 + 45 35 35 35 35 [국내선 항공] ㅇ 스칸디나비아 항공(Scandinavian Airlines), www.sas.dk ㅇ 그린란드 항공 (Air Greenland), http://airgreenland.com [자전거] ㅇ 수도 코펜하겐은 자전거 도로가 잘 정비되어 있으며 대다수의 시민의 자전거로 출퇴근을 함 ㅇ 모든 도심 도로는 자전거 2대가 다닐 수 있는 2.2m 넓이 자전거 전용도로가 설치돼 있으며 지하철역과 버스정류장 등 공공장소에는 자전거를 주차할 수 있는 시설이 설치되어 있음 ㅇ 자동차와 마찬가지로 자전거 도로의 왼쪽은 주로 추월차선으로 이용되므로 자전거 이용시 주의가 필요함 ㅇ 자전거에 대한 국제적인 관심 급증에 따라 2009년 덴마크는 세계 최초로 자전거 대사관(Cycling Embassy of Denmark)을 설립하였으며, 해당 대사관은 덴마크의 자전거 타기 문화를 국내외적으로 더 고취시키고 홍보하기 위한 사기업, 지방 정부, 비정부 조직들의 협력 공동체로 전 세계에 덴마크 자전거 문화를 알리는데 일조하고 있음

themes.heading: 기타 유의사항
themes.text: ㅇ 운전 또는 보행시 자전거 도로에 대한 인지가 중요함 ㅇ 주차시 차량내에 물건을 외부에서 노출되지 않는 곳에 두고 귀중품은 반드시 소지할 것 ㅇ 주차시 주차시작시간을 알리는 타이머가 보조석 앞 유리에 부착되어있으며, 주차가능시간이 제한된 무료 주차장에 주차할 경우 반드시 주차시작 시간을 맞춰야 함

themes.heading: 날씨
themes.text: ㅇ 덴마크는 북위 56도에 위치하여 평균적으로 추운 날씨지만 겨울에도 대체로 온화하며 연중 고른 강우량을 보이는 전형적인 해양성 기후입니다. 겨울에는 위도에 비해 온난하고, 여름에는 서늘해 연교차가 적으나 연중 바람이 많고 겨울에는 기후 변화가 심합니다. 5월부터 8월은 낮 시간이 거의 18시간 지속되지만 12월 즈음부터는 15시 - 16시면 해가 저물어 계절간 차이가 큽니다. ㅇ 날씨정보사이트 : www.dmi.dk(덴마크 기상청)

themes.heading: 기타
themes.text: [우리나라와의 시차] ㅇ 우리나라보다 8시간 느림 (서머타임 적용시 7시간) ※ 그린란드 12시간 느림 (서머타임 적용시 11시간) [지형] ㅇ 국토의 대부분이 스칸디나비아 산맥의 빙하의 운반, 퇴적에 의해 형성되어 평탄하고 (최고봉이 해발 173m) 호수가 많으며(1,008개), 하천은 규모도 작고(최장 158㎞) 많지 않은 편 ㅇ 주요 섬은 독일과 연결된 유틀란드(Jylland) 반도, 코펜하겐이 있는 셸란(Sjælland) 섬, 안데르센의 고향인 오덴세가 있는 퓐(Fyn) 섬

themes.heading: Country summary
themes.text: Reissued after periodic review with minor edits. Exercise increased caution in the Kingdom of Denmark due to terrorism. Country Summary: Terrorist groups continue plotting possible attacks in the Kingdom of Denmark. Terrorists can attack with little or no warning. They could target tourist spots, transport hubs, markets/shopping malls, local government facilities, hotels, clubs, restaurants, places of religious importance, parks, major sporting and cultural events, schools, airports, and other public areas. The Kingdom of Denmark consists of Denmark, the Faroe Islands, and Greenland. For additional information, please review the country information pages for Denmark and the Faroe Islands and for Greenland. If you decide to travel to the Kingdom of Denmark: Be aware of your surroundings when traveling to tourist locations and crowded public venues. Follow the instructions of local authorities. Monitor local media for breaking events and adjust your plans based on new information. Enroll in the Smart Traveler Enrollment Program (STEP) to receive Alerts and make it easier to locate you in an emergency. Follow the Department of State on Facebook and X/Twitter. Follow the U.S. Embassy in Copenhagen on Facebook and X/Twitter. Review the Country Security Report for Denmark. Prepare a contingency plan for emergency situations. Review the Traveler’s Checklist.

themes.heading: Reisehinweise (EDA)
themes.text: Dänemark Zuletzt editiert: 11/28/2025 2:15:45 PM Grundsätzliche Einschätzung Reisen nach Dänemark gelten grundsätzlich als sicher. In den grossen Städten kann es zu Demonstrationen kommen. Lassen Sie in der Umgebung von Demonstrationen Vorsicht walten, da Ausschreitungen möglich sind. Die dänischen Behörden verweisen auf die Bedrohung durch terroristische Anschläge. Lassen Sie Vorsicht walten, insbesondere an belebten Orten wie zum Beispiel: Restaurants, Einkaufszentren religiöse Einrichtungen Sport- und Kulturveranstaltungen Märkte, öffentliche Versammlungen Unterhaltungseinrichtungen und Nachtlokale Befolgen Sie die Empfehlungen der lokalen Behörden. Danish Security and Intelligence Service Die Rubrik Terrorismus und Entführungen macht auf die Risiken des Terrorismus aufmerksam. Terrorismus und Entführungen Spezifische regionale Risiken Färöer-Inseln: Bei länger andauernden Streiks können Versorgungsengpässe und Behinderungen des Reiseverkehrs nicht ausgeschlossen werden. Zum Beispiel führte ein mehrwöchiger Streik im Frühling 2024 zu Engpässen bei der Versorgung mit Treibstoff und Gütern des täglichen Bedarfs. Vertretung der Färöer-Inseln in Dänemark Visit Faroe Islands Grönland: Ausserhalb der grösseren Ortschaften ist die Infrastruktur stark eingeschränkt, auch für Such- und Rettungseinsätze. Während die medizinische Grundversorgung gewährleistet ist, müssen schwere Erkrankungen und Verletzungen in Dänemark behandelt werden. Zudem gibt es in abgelegenen Gebieten kein Mobilfunknetz. Aufgrund dieser Gegebenheiten und der extremen Witterungsbedingungen ist eine sorgfältige Vorbereitung sowie geeignete Ausrüstung erforderlich, etwa ein Navigationsgerät und ein Satellitentelefon. Befolgen Sie die Empfehlungen und Warnungen der lokalen Behörden und der Bevölkerung und lassen Sie sich bei Touren von einer lokalen Reiseleitung begleiten. Vertretung Grönlands in Dänemark Visit Greenland: Natural Hazards Kriminalität Besonders in öffentlichen Verkehrsmitteln, bei touristischen Attraktionen, am Hauptbahnhof Kopenhagen oder am Bahnhof Nørreport und in grösseren Menschenansammlungen kommt es vermehrt zu Taschendiebstählen. In den Städten Kopenhagen, Århus und Odense kann die Kriminalität je nach Stadtteil variieren. Schusswechsel zwischen verschiedenen kriminellen Banden sind möglich. In Kopenhagen sind insbesondere die Quartiere Nørrebro und Nordvest betroffen. Erkundigen Sie sich bei ortsansässigen Kontaktpersonen oder im Hotel, ob es Quartiere gibt, die – vor allem abends und nachts – besondere Vorsicht erfordern. Die dänische Polizei kann unter der Telefonnummer 112 kontaktiert werden. Beachten Sie im ganzen Land die üblichen Vorsichtsmassnahmen gegen Kleinkriminalität. Verkehr und Infrastruktur Das Strassennetz ist gut ausgebaut. Dänisches Aussenministerium: Auto und Verkehr Es besteht ein weit verzweigtes öffentliches Verkehrsnetz. Rejseplanen (Fahrplan) Besondere rechtliche Bestimmungen Informationen zu den Vorschriften im Strassenverkehr, u.a. zur zulässigen Promillegrenze, finden Sie unter Dänisches Aussenministerium: Auto und Verkehr Vergehen gegen das Betäubungsmittelgesetz werden schon bei geringen Mengen und bei jeder Art von Drogen bestraft. Naturbedingte Risiken Starke Regenfälle können Überschwemmungen, Erdrutsche und Infrastrukturschäden verursachen. Auch der Reiseverkehr kann vorübergehend beeinträchtigt werden. Beachten Sie die Wettervorhersagen sowie die Warnungen und Anweisungen der lokalen Behörden. Danish Meteorological Institute Danish Emergency Management Agency Medizinische Versorgung Die medizinische Versorgung ist gewährleistet. In Grönland ist diese jedoch eingeschränkt. Bei medizinischen Behandlungen muss die Europäische Krankenversicherungskarte vorgewiesen werden. Die Karte ist jedoch in Grönland und auf den Färöer-Inseln nicht gültig. Beachten Sie die Informationen von Gemeinsame Einrichtung KVG Wenn Sie auf bestimmte Medikamente angewiesen sind, sollte Ihre Reiseapotheke einen ausreichenden Vorrat enthalten. Bedenken Sie jedoch: In vielen Ländern gelten besondere Vorschriften für die Mitnahme von betäubungsmittelhaltigen Medikamenten (z.B. Methadon) und Substanzen, mit denen psychische Erkrankungen behandelt werden. Erkundigen Sie sich gegebenenfalls vor der Abreise direkt bei der zuständigen ausländischen Vertretung (Botschaft oder Konsulat) und konsultieren Sie die Rubrik Reiselinks, wo Sie unter anderem weitere Informationen zu diesem Thema sowie generell zur Reisemedizin finden. Über die Verbreitung von Krankheiten und mögliche Schutzmassnahmen informieren medizinische Fachpersonen und Impfzentren. Reiselinks Besondere Hinweise Sie sind verpflichtet, sich jederzeit mit dem Pass oder der Identitätskarte ausweisen zu können. Nützliche Adressen Allgemeine Notruf-Nummer: 112 Dänisches Aussenministerium: Reise und Aufenthalt Zollvorschriften Dänemark Schweizer Vertretungen im Ausland: Wenn Sie im Ausland in eine Notlage geraten, können Sie sich an die nächste Schweizer Vertretung oder an die Helpline EDA wenden. Regionales Konsularcenter Nordische und Baltische Staaten in Stockholm, Schweden Schweizerische Botschaft in Dänemark Helpline EDA Ausländische Vertretungen in der Schweiz: Auskunft über die Einreisevorschriften (zugelassene Ausweise, Visum etc.) erteilen die zuständigen ausländischen Botschaften und Konsulate. Sie informieren auch über die Zollbestimmungen für die Ein- und Ausfuhr von Tieren und Waren: elektronische Geräte, Souvenirs, Medikamente etc. Ausländische Vertretungen in der Schweiz Ausschluss der Haftung Ausschluss der Haftung Die Reisehinweise des Eidgenössischen Departements für auswärtige Angelegenheiten (EDA) stützen sich auf eigene, als vertrauenswürdig eingeschätzte Informationsquellen. Sie verstehen sich als nützliche Hinweise zur Planung einer Reise. Das EDA kann Reisenden aber den Entscheid und die Verantwortung für die Vorbereitung und Durchführung der Reise nicht abnehmen. Gefahrensituationen sind oft nicht vorhersehbar, unübersichtlich und können sich rasch ändern. Das EDA übernimmt keine Gewähr für die Vollständigkeit der Reisehinweise und für die Richtigkeit des Inhalts von verlinkten externen Internetseiten. Es lehnt jede Haftung für allfällige Schäden im Zusammenhang mit einer Reise ab. Forderungen im Zusammenhang mit der Annullierung einer Reise sind direkt beim Reisebüro oder der Reiseversicherung geltend zu machen.
